sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻) and dual descriptor for pyridine; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ on the most reactive atom.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: single-point fukui-reactivity calculation on the user-supplied pyridine.xyz geometry; no further optimisation (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge –1, multiplicity 2) states were generated internally for the finite-difference Fukui analysis.
Software & version: chemkit 0.x calling PySCF 2.x.
Convergence & warnings: SCF calculations for the N, N–1, and N+1 states all converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.  Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "common header present"
All checks passed; charge conservation warnings simply note the expected ∑f±≈1.0 and are informational.

Key results (Mulliken-based indices):
Most electrophilic site: atom 1 (N), f⁺ = 0.48422572
Most nucleophilic site: atom 4 (C4), f⁻ = 0.11826260
Dual descriptor highlights N (0.42010305) as the dominant electrophilic center and hydrogens as nucleophilic.

Reproducibility
Command executed:
cli … fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz
Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp5x1k_j5i/live.json
Plot image: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/argo_o3/20260704-170413_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
The live PySCF output was streamed during the run to the .out log announced at launch.

Headline value
f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index) on the most reactive atom (N, atom 1): 0.48422572 (dimensionless).
